quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Strait of Juan de Fuca was named after a Greek sailor who sailed under a Spanish name! His real name was Ioannis Phokas, but he became known as Juan de Fuca when he claimed to have sailed through this waterway in 1592. The strait is one of the world's most important marine highways, connecting the Pacific Ocean to the Salish Sea.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Flowers in Victorian Gardens
short_read.teaser: Victorians used flowers to send coded messages of love, friendship, and even warnings. Discover what your favorite garden blooms might have "said" in the 1800s.

medium_read.title: The Remarkable Return of Gray Whales to the Pacific Coast
medium_read.teaser: Gray whales make one of the longest migrations of any mammal, traveling from Alaska to Mexico and back each year. Learn about their incredible comeback from near extinction and how to spot them along the Washington coast.

long_read.title: The Wild Olympics: America's Last Untouched Rainforest
long_read.teaser: The Olympic Peninsula holds one of North America's few temperate rainforests, where moss-draped trees tower over valleys that receive over 12 feet of rain annually. Explore the unique ecosystem that makes this corner of Washington so magical.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Ediz Hook
port_angeles.description: Ediz Hook, the natural sand spit protecting Port Angeles Harbor, is home to a thriving population of Columbian black-tailed deer. These gentle creatures are often spotted grazing near the beach in the early morning or evening hours. The hook is also a wonderful spot for birdwatching and enjoying views of the Strait and the mountains.

puzzle_1.question: Olympic National Park is home to over ___ miles of wilderness coastline.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Perfect! Olympic National Park's 73 miles of wild coastline is one of its greatest treasures!
puzzle_1.close_response: Very close! The park protects 73 miles of beautiful, rugged coastline.
puzzle_1.wrong_response: The park actually has 73 miles of pristine coastline — the longest stretch of undeveloped coast in the continental United States!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Olympic National Park's coastal section features dramatic sea stacks, tide pools teeming with life, and beaches where you can see bald eagles soaring overhead. It's one of the most pristine coastal wilderness areas in the lower 48 states.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! 'Pillangó' is such a beautiful word for such a beautiful creature!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó' — doesn't it sound delicate and light, just like a butterfly's wings?
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungary is home to over 200 species of butterflies, including the stunning Swallowtail. The word 'pillangó' comes from an old Hungarian root meaning something that flutters or flits about.

puzzle_3.question: What unique feature do sea otters have that helps them stay warm in cold Pacific waters?
options: The densest fur of any animal
They migrate to warmer waters in winter
puzzle_3.correct_response: Exactly right! Sea otters have the densest fur of any animal on Earth — up to one million hairs per square inch! This incredible coat keeps them cozy in our chilly waters.
puzzle_3.wrong_response: Sea otters actually have the densest fur of any animal — nearly one million hairs per square inch! Unlike seals and whales, they don't have blubber, so that luxurious fur is their warm winter coat.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Sea otters spend much of their day grooming their fur to maintain its insulating properties. You can often spot them floating on their backs in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, using their bellies as dinner tables while they crack open shellfish!

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
